Class Game

java.lang.Object
it.polimi.Game.Core.Game
public class Game extends Object
Main model class representing the state of a Mesos game session. Manages players, card decks, board rows, and the progression of rounds and eras.

  • Field Summary

    Fields
    Modifier and Type
    Field
    Description
    static final int
    MAX_ROUNDS
    Total number of rounds in a standard Mesos game.

    Modifier and Type
    Method
    Description
    void
    addPropertyChangeListener(PropertyChangeListener listener)
    Registers a listener that will be notified when the game state changes.
    void
    chooseOfferTile(Player player, OfferTile tile)
    Assigns the chosen offer tile to the player while keeping it visible in the offer track.
    void
    executeRound()
    Executes a full round: prepares tiles, resolves the first round if needed, updates rows, and advances the round counter.
    void
    executeTurn(Player player, List<Tribe> upperChoices, List<Tribe> lowerChoices)
    Validates a player's selected cards and applies the turn to the game state.
    void
    finishRoundAfterPlayerActions()
    Completes a round after every player has resolved their offer tile action.
    Era
    getCurrentEra()
    Returns the current era of the game.
    List<Building>
    getLowerBuildings()
    Returns the buildings currently visible in the lower row.
    List<Tribe>
    getLowerRow()
    Returns the cards currently visible in the lower row.
    List<OfferTile>
    getOfferTiles()
    Returns the offer tiles currently available to players.
    List<Player>
    getPlayers()
    Returns the list of registered players.
    int
    getRound()
    Returns the current round number.
    Stack<Tribe>
    getTribeDeck()
    Returns the remaining tribe deck.
    List<Building>
    getUpperBuildings()
    Returns the buildings currently visible in the upper row.
    List<Tribe>
    getUpperRow()
    Returns the cards currently visible in the upper row.
    void
    handleEraChange(Era newEra)
    Updates the current era and applies the start-of-era building steps.
    void
    initialize(int numPlayers)
    Initializes the game state for the given number of players.
    void
    initialize(int numPlayers, List<Color> playerColors)
    Initializes the game state for the given players using the provided player colors.
    void
    manageCards()
    Moves cards between rows and draws new cards to keep the table balanced.
    void
    notifyNotification(String message)
    Broadcasts a non-state-critical message to all subscribed views.
    void
    prepareFirstRound()
    Builds the initial table layout for the first round.
    void
    prepareOfferTiles()
    Loads the available offer tiles and keeps only the ones valid for the current player count.
    void
    removePropertyChangeListener(PropertyChangeListener listener)
    Removes a previously registered game state listener.
    void
    resolveEndOfRoundEvents()
    Resolves end-of-round event effects.
    Building
    takeLowerBuildingCard(Player player, int index)
    Takes one building from the lower building row by index, paying its food cost.
    Tribe
    takeLowerTribeCard(Player player, int index)
    Takes one character card from the lower tribe row by index.
    Building
    takeUpperBuildingCard(Player player, int index)
    Takes one building from the upper building row by index, paying its food cost.
    Tribe
    takeUpperTribeCard(Player player, int index)
    Takes one character card from the upper tribe row by index.
    String
    toString()
    Returns a compact textual summary of the current game state.

  • Field Details

    • MAX_ROUNDS

      public static final int MAX_ROUNDS
      Total number of rounds in a standard Mesos game.
      See Also:
      • Constant Field Values

  • Constructor Details

    • Game

      public Game()
      Constructs a new Game instance with default initial values.

  • Method Details

    • addPropertyChangeListener

      public void addPropertyChangeListener(PropertyChangeListener listener)
      Registers a listener that will be notified when the game state changes.
      Parameters:
      listener - The listener to add.

    • notifyNotification

      public void notifyNotification(String message)
      Broadcasts a non-state-critical message to all subscribed views. This is used for game notifications (e.g., end-of-game final scores).
      Parameters:
      message - message to broadcast (ignored if null/blank)

    • removePropertyChangeListener

      public void removePropertyChangeListener(PropertyChangeListener listener)
      Removes a previously registered game state listener.
      Parameters:
      listener - The listener to remove.

    • initialize

      public void initialize(int numPlayers) throws Exception
      Initializes the game state for the given number of players. Loads cards, creates the deck, and prepares the initial round data.
      Parameters:
      numPlayers - The number of players (2-5).
      Throws:
      Exception - If card loading fails.
      IllegalArgumentException - If numPlayers is outside the allowed range.

    • initialize

      public void initialize(int numPlayers, List<Color> playerColors) throws Exception
      Initializes the game state for the given players using the provided player colors.
      Parameters:
      numPlayers - The number of players (2-5).
      playerColors - The colors selected by players, in player order.
      Throws:
      Exception - If card loading fails.
      IllegalArgumentException - If numPlayers or playerColors are invalid.

    • executeRound

      public void executeRound() throws Exception
      Executes a full round: prepares tiles, resolves the first round if needed, updates rows, and advances the round counter.
      Throws:
      Exception - If preparing offer tiles fails.

    • finishRoundAfterPlayerActions

      public void finishRoundAfterPlayerActions()
      Completes a round after every player has resolved their offer tile action. End-of-round events are resolved before the lower row is discarded and before the upper row slides down.

    • prepareFirstRound

      public void prepareFirstRound()
      Builds the initial table layout for the first round.

    • manageCards

      public void manageCards()
      Moves cards between rows and draws new cards to keep the table balanced.

    • chooseOfferTile

      public void chooseOfferTile(Player player, OfferTile tile)
      Assigns the chosen offer tile to the player while keeping it visible in the offer track.
      Parameters:
      player - The player choosing the tile.
      tile - The selected offer tile.
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - If the tile is not available.

    • prepareOfferTiles

      public void prepareOfferTiles() throws Exception
      Loads the available offer tiles and keeps only the ones valid for the current player count.
      Throws:
      Exception - If card loading fails.

    • executeTurn

      public void executeTurn(Player player, List<Tribe> upperChoices, List<Tribe> lowerChoices)
      Validates a player's selected cards and applies the turn to the game state.
      Parameters:
      player - The player executing the turn.
      upperChoices - Cards selected from the upper row.
      lowerChoices - Cards selected from the lower row.
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - If the selections are invalid.

    • resolveEndOfRoundEvents

      public void resolveEndOfRoundEvents()
      Resolves end-of-round event effects.

    • handleEraChange

      public void handleEraChange(Era newEra)
      Updates the current era and applies the start-of-era building steps.

      When entering Era II or Era III:

      1. (Era III only) discard any buildings in the lower row
      2. move all upper-row buildings to the lower row
      3. reveal the new era's buildings in the upper row

      Parameters:
      newEra - The new era to transition to.

    • getUpperBuildings

      public List<Building> getUpperBuildings()
      Returns the buildings currently visible in the upper row.

    • getLowerBuildings

      public List<Building> getLowerBuildings()
      Returns the buildings currently visible in the lower row.

    • takeUpperTribeCard

      public Tribe takeUpperTribeCard(Player player, int index)
      Takes one character card from the upper tribe row by index.

    • takeLowerTribeCard

      public Tribe takeLowerTribeCard(Player player, int index)
      Takes one character card from the lower tribe row by index.

    • takeUpperBuildingCard

      public Building takeUpperBuildingCard(Player player, int index)
      Takes one building from the upper building row by index, paying its food cost.

    • takeLowerBuildingCard

      public Building takeLowerBuildingCard(Player player, int index)
      Takes one building from the lower building row by index, paying its food cost.

    • getTribeDeck

      public Stack<Tribe> getTribeDeck()
      Returns the remaining tribe deck.
      Returns:
      The stack of tribe cards.

    • getRound

      public int getRound()
      Returns the current round number.
      Returns:
      The current round.

    • getCurrentEra

      public Era getCurrentEra()
      Returns the current era of the game.
      Returns:
      The current era.

    • getUpperRow

      public List<Tribe> getUpperRow()
      Returns the cards currently visible in the upper row.
      Returns:
      List of tribe cards in the upper row.

    • getLowerRow

      public List<Tribe> getLowerRow()
      Returns the cards currently visible in the lower row.
      Returns:
      List of tribe cards in the lower row.

    • getPlayers

      public List<Player> getPlayers()
      Returns the list of registered players.
      Returns:
      List of players.

    • getOfferTiles

      public List<OfferTile> getOfferTiles()
      Returns the offer tiles currently available to players.
      Returns:
      List of available offer tiles.

    • toString

      public String toString()
      Returns a compact textual summary of the current game state.
      Overrides:
      toString in class Object
      Returns:
      A string representation of the game state.
